metadata.query_text: skin lesion multi-class classification small sample imbalanced dermatoscopy

raw_candidates.snippet: Deep convolutional neural network (DCNN) models have been widely explored for skin disease diagnosis and some of them have achieved the diagnostic outcomes comparable or even superior to those of dermatologists. However, broad implementation of DCNN in skin disease detection is hindered by small size and data imbalance of the publically accessible skin lesion datasets. This paper proposes a novel single-model based strategy for classification of skin lesions on small and imbalanced datasets. First, various DCNNs are trained on different small and imbalanced datasets to verify that the models with moderate complexity outperform the larger models. Second, regularization DropOut and DropBlock are added to reduce overfitting and a Modified RandAugment augmentation strategy is proposed to deal with the defects of sample underrepresentation in the small dataset. Finally, a novel Multi-Weighted New Loss (MWNL) function and an end-to-end cumulative learning strategy (CLS) are introduced to overcome the challenge of uneven sample size and classification difficulty and to reduce the impact of abnormal samples on training. By combining Modified RandAugment, MWNL and CLS, our single DCNN model method achieved the classification accuracy comparable or superior to those of multiple ensembling models on different dermoscopic image datasets. Our study shows that this method is able to achieve a high classification performance at a low cost of computational resources and inference time, potentially suitable to implement in mobile devices for automated screening of skin lesions and many other malignancies in low resource settings.
raw_candidates.title: Single Model Deep Learning on Imbalanced Small Datasets for Skin Lesion Classification

metadata.query_text: skin lesion multi-class classification small sample imbalanced dermatoscopy

raw_candidates.snippet: Early and precise diagnosis of skin cancer is essential to proper and improved treatment and patient outcomes. Traditional convolutional neural networks (CNNs) are known to be very effective in classifying skin lesions but they need large labelled datasets and have difficulty in capturing the global context data. This paper is an attempt to overcome these limitations by introducing a self-trained Vision Transformer (ViT-S/16) using the Distillation with NO labels (DINO) architecture to classify multi-class skin cancer. The pretrained DINO-ViT-S/16 model was fine-tuned using a highly imbalanced dataset, HAM10000, on which some lesion classes have only a number of samples, which poses a risk of overfitting. To overcome this, there was the application of a dynamic on-the-fly augmentation and image-weighting strategy apllied to mitigate it and stratified 5-fold cross-validation used for robust evaluation. Through experimental results, it is shown that the proposed model achieved an accuracy, precision, recall, and F1-score of 95.86%, 95.30%, 95.44%, and 95.88% respectively, compared to the traditional CNN, pretrained CNN (ResNet50, EfficientNet, and Xception) and Vision Transformer baseline. Such results demonstrate the usefulness of self-supervised DINO Vision Transformers to learn powerful feature representations of dermatological images, which is a promising strategy in diagnosing skin cancer with high accuracy, in limited amounts of data, and with balance.
raw_candidates.title: Enhancing Multi-class Skin Cancer Image Classification Using Self-supervised Vision Transformer Architecture

metadata.query_text: transfer learning skin lesion classification imbalanced data ISIC

raw_candidates.snippet: Accurate skin lesion classification can benefit from lesion segmentation masks, but requiring masks or an auxiliary segmentation model during inference reduces clinical practicality and increases computational complexity. This work introduces Privileged Lesion-Context Relational Distillation (PLCRD), a teacher-student framework that exploits lesion masks exclusively during training while preserving image-only inference. The privileged teacher jointly analyzes the original dermoscopic image and its mask-guided lesion region to learn lesion-specific and contextual diagnostic representations. An image-only student is then trained through complementary knowledge-transfer mechanisms that convey the teacher's diagnostic distribution, lesion-focused attention, inter-lesion relational geometry, and lesion-context structure. PLCRD decomposes deep representations into lesion and contextual embeddings and transfers their relational organization through inter-lesion similarity alignment, lesion-context affinity matching, separation regularization, and class-aware relational learning. This formulation avoids direct feature matching between heterogeneous teacher and student architectures and enables the student to internalize mask-informed diagnostic structure without accessing masks at deployment. The framework was evaluated on HAM10000 using lesion-disjoint data partitioning and externally validated on ISIC 2018 without retraining. PLCRD achieved a lesion-level macro-F1 of 0.773 +/- 0.018, balanced accuracy of 0.764 +/- 0.023, and macro-AUROC of 0.976 +/- 0.002 on HAM10000, together with a macro-F1 of 0.732 +/- 0.008 on ISIC 2018. The results indicate that privileged lesion annotations can be transformed into transferable relational knowledge, yielding a practical and interpretable approach to mask-free skin lesion classification.
raw_candidates.title: Privileged Lesion-Context Relational Distillation for Mask-Free Skin Lesion Classification

metadata.query_text: dermoscopy class imbalance bias mitigation strategy multi-class classification

raw_candidates.snippet: Accurate multi-class classification of skin lesions remains a challenging task due to the intrinsic complexity of dermoscopic images and the severe class imbalance present in medical datasets. This paper introduces an ensemble-based deep learning pipeline designed to achieve robust and generalizable multi-class skin lesion diagnosis. The proposed framework systematically addresses the primary challenges of class imbalance using an aggressive mix up augmentation. A five face structured pipeline encompassing data preparation, stratified crossvalidation, model training, ensemble inference, and performance evaluation is performed. The proposed ensemble integrates five heterogeneous deep architectures: Vision Transformer (ViT), Swin Transformer, ConvNeXt, EfficientNet, and DenseNet, each contributing complementary representational strengths. To enhance model robustness and fairness, the pipeline incorporates regularization and a hybrid loss combining focal Loss and label smoothing. Ensemble predictions are aggregated using a weighted soft voting strategy, ensuring stable and accurate classification outcomes across all lesion categories. Experimental evaluations demonstrate that the proposed system achieves state-of-the-art performance on the HAM10000 dataset, delivering high accuracy and balanced sensitivity across minority classes. The end-to-end design emphasizes reproducibility, scalability, and transparency, establishing a robust foundation for future research in automated dermatological diagnosis.
raw_candidates.title: Ensemble-Based Deep Learning Framework for Multi-Class Skin Lesion Diagnosis with Class Imbalance Mitigation

metadata.query_text: dermoscopy class imbalance bias mitigation strategy multi-class classification

raw_candidates.snippet: Multi-class building change detection in urban areas is critical for urban monitoring, disaster management, and planning. This study focuses on improving multi-class change detection performance in high-resolution aerial imagery by addressing the challenges of data imbalance and leveraging a Visual State Space Model (VSSM)-based architecture. To mitigate class imbalance, we introduced object-based random cropping and dynamic class weighting, which enhanced the representation of underrepresented classes during training. These strategies, combined with the robust spatio-temporal modeling capabilities of VSSMs, enabled accurate classification of changes such as new construction, demolition, renovation, color change, and no change. The proposed approach achieved balanced performance across all categories, with a mean Intersection over Union (mIoU) of 0.575 and Cohen's Kappa Coefficient (Kappa) of 0.691, demonstrating its effectiveness in multi-class building change detection tasks in complex urban environments.
raw_candidates.title: Multi-Class Building Change Detection in Urban Areas Using High-Resolution Aerial Images with Class Imbalance Mitigation and VSSM-Based Model

gap_assessments.summary: Multiple studies demonstrate effective strategies for skin lesion multi-class classification under small sample and imbalanced conditions, including single-model approaches with regularization and augmentation, self-supervised Vision Transformers, and ensemble-based pipelines.

limitations: Effectiveness of class weighting and augmentation may depend on dataset-specific characteristics.
Some methods (e.g., mix-up) may introduce synthetic samples that do not reflect true clinical variability.
gap_assessments.summary: Class imbalance mitigation strategies are well-documented across multiple approaches, including dynamic augmentation, class weighting, focal loss, label smoothing, and ensemble-based methods, with reported improvements in balanced accuracy and minority class performance.

limitations: Stratified cross-validation may not fully capture real-world data drift or domain shift.
External validation is limited to specific datasets (e.g., ISIC 2018), and performance may not generalize to other clinical settings.
gap_assessments.summary: Robust evaluation frameworks using stratified cross-validation, lesion-disjoint partitioning, and external validation (e.g., ISIC 2018) are employed to assess model generalizability and reduce bias in imbalanced datasets.

limitations: Interpretability methods (e.g., attention maps) may not always align with clinical reasoning or provide causal explanations.
Mask-free approaches (e.g., PLCRD) rely on lesion-context relational knowledge transfer, which may not be universally applicable to all architectures.
gap_assessments.summary: Interpretable and practical model designs are proposed, including mask-free inference via privileged knowledge distillation, attention mechanisms, and relational learning to enhance clinical interpretability without sacrificing performance.

limitations: Generalization to external clinical datasets beyond HAM10000 and ISIC 2018 is not fully validated in all studies.
Interpretability methods may not provide clinically actionable explanations for all model decisions.
Performance metrics (e.g., macro-F1, balanced accuracy) are dataset-dependent and may not reflect real-world clinical utility.
Some methods (e.g., privileged knowledge distillation) require specific architectural designs that may limit flexibility.

verified_findings.text: Single-model deep learning approaches with moderate complexity can achieve classification performance comparable to or superior to larger models on small and imbalanced skin lesion datasets.
Self-supervised Vision Transformers (e.g., DINO-ViT-S/16) fine-tuned on imbalanced datasets can achieve high accuracy (e.g., 95.86% accuracy) and robust performance across minority classes.

Privileged Lesion-Context Relational Distillation (PLCRD) enables mask-free inference while preserving lesion-specific diagnostic knowledge, achieving macro-F1 of 0.773 on HAM10000 and 0.732 on ISIC 2018.

Ensemble-based pipelines integrating heterogeneous architectures (e.g., ViT, Swin Transformer, ConvNeXt, EfficientNet, DenseNet) with focal loss and label smoothing can mitigate class imbalance and achieve state-of-the-art performance on HAM10000.

Class imbalance mitigation strategies such as dynamic augmentation, class weighting, and hybrid loss functions improve balanced accuracy and sensitivity for minority classes in multi-class skin lesion classification.

Stratified cross-validation and lesion-disjoint partitioning are effective for robust evaluation of skin lesion classification models, reducing overfitting and improving generalization to external datasets.

metadata.query_text: "Oriented R-CNN for Object Detection" official implementation code repository

items.summary: Current state-of-the-art two-stage detectors generate oriented proposals through time-consuming schemes. This diminishes the detectors' speed, thereby becoming the computational bottleneck in advanced oriented object detection systems. This work proposes an effective and simple oriented object detection framework, termed Oriented R-CNN, which is a general two-stage oriented detector with promising accuracy and efficiency. To be specific, in the first stage, we propose an oriented Region Proposal Network (oriented RPN) that directly generates high-quality oriented proposals in a nearly cost-free manner. The second stage is oriented R-CNN head for refining oriented Regions of Interest (oriented RoIs) and recognizing them. Without tricks, oriented R-CNN with ResNet50 achieves state-of-the-art detection accuracy on two commonly-used datasets for oriented object detection including DOTA (75.87% mAP) and HRSC2016 (96.50% mAP), while having a speed of 15.1 FPS with the image size of 1024$\times$1024 on a single RTX 2080Ti. We hope our work could inspire rethinking the design of oriented detectors and serve as a baseline for oriented object detection. Code is available at https://github.com/jbwang1997/OBBDetection.
items.title: Oriented R-CNN for Object Detection

method.falsifiable_hypothesis: Under 用户已明确以 Oriented R-CNN 为基线，且其实现与评测协议已冻结并可复现。, 在 oriented R-CNN head 中插入 RFPP 模块，替换原有的标准 RoIAlign 操作。RFPP 采用旋转不变的特征聚合（如旋转自适应池化）与稀疏采样策略，在保持旋转框检测精度的前提下，降低高分辨率图像的推理延迟与内存占用。 should 在 DOTA 密集小目标场景中，高分辨率图像（1024×1024 及以上）的推理延迟预计降低 25–35%，内存占用降低 20–30%，同时保持 mAP 不下降（相对基线波动 <±0.5%）。 because Oriented R-CNN 的 RoIAlign 操作在旋转框检测中需对齐旋转角度，导致特征采样点数量与旋转角度相关，高分辨率下计算与内存开销显著。RFPP 通过旋转不变的特征金字塔池化，将旋转对齐操作前移至特征层，减少后续 RoIAlign 的计算负担，同时保持旋转框检测精度。 addresses RFPP 模块的有效性依赖于 Oriented R-CNN 的 oriented RoI 提案质量；若提案质量较差（如密集小目标漏检），RFPP 的精度保持能力将受限。, while RFPP 模块的旋转不变特征聚合必须保证旋转角度估计的鲁棒性；若旋转角度估计误差过大，可能导致特征对齐失败，进而影响检测精度。因此，需在 RFPP 中集成旋转角度估计的置信度阈值机制，确保仅在高置信度下应用旋转对齐。.

integration_contracts.input: 输入为 Oriented R-CNN 的特征图（任意分辨率）与旋转框 RoI（x, y, w, h, θ）。输出为旋转对齐后的 RoI 特征图，尺寸固定且与旋转角度无关。
integration_contracts.output: 输出为与输入 RoI 对应的旋转对齐特征图，尺寸为 (7×7×C)，其中 C 为通道数。该特征图可直接输入 oriented R-CNN head 的后续分类与回归层。

supported_claims: Oriented R-CNN directly generates high-quality oriented proposals in a nearly cost-free manner, reducing the computational bottleneck in oriented object detection systems.
Oriented R-CNN with ResNet50 achieves 75.87% mAP on the DOTA dataset and 96.50% mAP on the HRSC2016 dataset.
Oriented R-CNN with ResNet50 processes 1024×1024 images at 15.1 FPS on a single RTX 2080Ti.
The official implementation of Oriented R-CNN is available at https://github.com/jbwang1997/OBBDetection.
evidence.title: Oriented R-CNN for Object Detection

supported_claims: The official implementation of Oriented R-CNN is available at https://github.com/jbwang1997/OBBDetection.

hypothesis.condition: 用户已明确以 Oriented R-CNN 为基线，且其实现与评测协议已冻结并可复现。
hypothesis.guardrail: RFPP 模块的旋转不变特征聚合必须保证旋转角度估计的鲁棒性；若旋转角度估计误差过大，可能导致特征对齐失败，进而影响检测精度。因此，需在 RFPP 中集成旋转角度估计的置信度阈值机制，确保仅在高置信度下应用旋转对齐。
hypothesis.intervention: 在 oriented R-CNN head 中插入 RFPP 模块，替换原有的标准 RoIAlign 操作。RFPP 采用旋转不变的特征聚合（如旋转自适应池化）与稀疏采样策略，在保持旋转框检测精度的前提下，降低高分辨率图像的推理延迟与内存占用。
hypothesis.limitation: RFPP 模块的有效性依赖于 Oriented R-CNN 的 oriented RoI 提案质量；若提案质量较差（如密集小目标漏检），RFPP 的精度保持能力将受限。
hypothesis.mechanism: Oriented R-CNN 的 RoIAlign 操作在旋转框检测中需对齐旋转角度，导致特征采样点数量与旋转角度相关，高分辨率下计算与内存开销显著。RFPP 通过旋转不变的特征金字塔池化，将旋转对齐操作前移至特征层，减少后续 RoIAlign 的计算负担，同时保持旋转框检测精度。
hypothesis.predicted_metric_change: 在 DOTA 密集小目标场景中，高分辨率图像（1024×1024 及以上）的推理延迟预计降低 25–35%，内存占用降低 20–30%，同时保持 mAP 不下降（相对基线波动 <±0.5%）。

assumptions: Oriented R-CNN 在 DOTA 上的基线精度和推理成本数值可能未在公开文献中明确报告，或仅以图表形式呈现，需进一步确认。
现有降低高分辨率推理成本的方法可能主要针对水平框检测（HBB），而非旋转框检测（OBB），适用性存在不确定性。
DOTA 数据集版本（v1.0/v2.0）的选择会影响结果可比性，需在检索时明确版本信息。
高分辨率推理成本与精度的权衡曲线可能依赖于具体硬件平台（如 GPU 型号、内存大小），公开文献中的数值可能不具备直接可比性。
the selected insertion point preserves representation semantics
modules.baseline_parity_behavior: when disabled, the module contributes no parameters, operations, preprocessing, or loss terms
modules.compute_cost: RFPP 模块的计算开销主要集中在旋转不变特征聚合与稀疏采样，预计在高分辨率图像上引入的额外计算开销低于 10%，但通过减少后续 RoIAlign 的计算负担，整体推理延迟仍有显著降低。
modules.failure_mode: 旋转角度估计误差过大导致 RFPP 特征对齐失败，进而引发检测精度下降；或 RFPP 的稀疏采样策略过于激进，导致关键目标特征丢失，影响小目标检测性能。
modules.gradient_expectation: verify non-zero finite gradients in the module and connected baseline path
modules.input_semantics: 输入为 Oriented R-CNN 的特征图（任意分辨率）与旋转框 RoI（x, y, w, h, θ）。输出为旋转对齐后的 RoI 特征图，尺寸固定且与旋转角度无关。
modules.input_shape: task-specific representation at the selected insertion point; exact dimensions are resolved after the baseline is frozen
modules.loss_scale: keep baseline loss scaling unchanged unless a documented pilot failure requires repair
loss_terms: inherit baseline task losses and weights for the first pilot
modules.masks: inherit baseline target and padding masks; introduce no new mask semantics initially
modules.name: rotated_feature_pyramid_pooling
modules.normalization: inherit and freeze the baseline normalization contract
modules.ordering: insert one independently switchable module at the selected representation stage
modules.original_role: Oriented R-CNN head 中的 RoIAlign 操作，用于从特征图中提取旋转框对应的旋转区域特征。
modules.output_semantics: 输出为与输入 RoI 对应的旋转对齐特征图，尺寸为 (7×7×C)，其中 C 为通道数。该特征图可直接输入 oriented R-CNN head 的后续分类与回归层。
modules.output_shape: representation contract required by the downstream baseline stage
modules.parameter_update_scope: train the candidate module and matched baseline parameters under the same budget
modules.predicted_effect: 在保持 DOTA mAP 基线（75.87%）的前提下，高分辨率图像（≥1024×1024）的推理延迟与内存占用显著降低，适用于密集小目标场景的实时或准实时部署需求。
modules.proposed_role: 替换 RoIAlign，作为旋转感知的特征下采样与对齐模块，在保持旋转框检测精度的同时，降低高分辨率推理成本。

constraints: Oriented R-CNN 在 DOTA 上的基线精度和推理成本数值可能未在公开文献中明确报告，或仅以图表形式呈现，需进一步确认。
现有降低高分辨率推理成本的方法可能主要针对水平框检测（HBB），而非旋转框检测（OBB），适用性存在不确定性。
DOTA 数据集版本（v1.0/v2.0）的选择会影响结果可比性，需在检索时明确版本信息。

research.intended_claim: 在 Oriented R-CNN 的 oriented R-CNN head 中引入旋转感知的特征下采样与对齐模块（Rotated Feature Pyramid Pooling, RFPP），通过旋转不变的特征聚合与稀疏采样策略，在保持旋转框检测精度的同时，降低高分辨率图像推理的计算与内存开销。RFPP 独立于 oriented RPN，可作为可切换的插件模块部署。
research.observed_problem: RFPP 模块的有效性依赖于 Oriented R-CNN 的 oriented RoI 提案质量；若提案质量较差（如密集小目标漏检），RFPP 的精度保持能力将受限。
research.proposed_mechanism: Oriented R-CNN 的 RoIAlign 操作在旋转框检测中需对齐旋转角度，导致特征采样点数量与旋转角度相关，高分辨率下计算与内存开销显著。RFPP 通过旋转不变的特征金字塔池化，将旋转对齐操作前移至特征层，减少后续 RoIAlign 的计算负担，同时保持旋转框检测精度。
research.scientific_setting: 面向 DOTA 数据集（v1.0/v2.0）的旋转框目标检测任务，聚焦密集小目标（如飞机、船只等）的高分辨率图像（如 1024×1024 及以上）推理优化，不涉及其他数据集或任务变体。
research.success_metric: DOTA 数据集（v1.0/v2.0）上的 mAP（mean Average Precision）与推理延迟（FPS）。
research.target_problem: 以论文《Oriented R-CNN for Object Detection》为 Baseline，在 DOTA 密集小目标场景中降低高分辨率推理成本，同时保持旋转框检测精度。

stop_conditions: 当 RFPP 模块在验证集上 mAP 相对基线下降超过 0.5%，或推理延迟未达到预期降低幅度（<20%）时，终止实验并回滚至基线配置。
stop if the target gain disappears under matched data and tuning
revise if latency, memory, or compute guardrails fail on the selected device
do not claim novelty if a stronger comparison implements the same mechanism

modules.name: rotated_feature_pyramid_pooling
modules.purpose: 替换 RoIAlign，作为旋转感知的特征下采样与对齐模块，在保持旋转框检测精度的同时，降低高分辨率推理成本。

method.problem_method_insight: Oriented R-CNN 的 oriented RPN 在 DOTA 密集小目标场景中生成高质量旋转框提案的计算开销相对较低，但其 RoI 池化与 RoIAlign 操作在高分辨率图像（≥1024×1024）上的特征采样与旋转对齐仍是推理延迟与内存占用的主要来源。现有方法未针对旋转框检测的特征对齐与采样瓶颈提供显式优化，导致高分辨率推理成本居高不下。
risks: Oriented R-CNN 在 DOTA 上的基线精度和推理成本数值可能未在公开文献中明确报告，或仅以图表形式呈现，需进一步确认。
现有降低高分辨率推理成本的方法可能主要针对水平框检测（HBB），而非旋转框检测（OBB），适用性存在不确定性。
DOTA 数据集版本（v1.0/v2.0）的选择会影响结果可比性，需在检索时明确版本信息。
高分辨率推理成本与精度的权衡曲线可能依赖于具体硬件平台（如 GPU 型号、内存大小），公开文献中的数值可能不具备直接可比性。
在 DOTA 密集小目标场景中，是否需要优先保证特定类别（如飞机、船只等）的检测精度，还是整体 mAP 为主要优化目标？

evidence_gaps.description: Verify the public identity, method details, declared role, and task compatibility of the user-supplied material titled 'Oriented R-CNN for Object Detection'.
确定 Oriented R-CNN 在 DOTA 数据集上的基线精度（mAP）和推理成本（FLOPs/参数量/延迟）的具体数值范围，以作为后续优化的对比基准。
识别高分辨率推理成本的主要来源（如特征提取、RoI Pooling、旋转框回归等）在 Oriented R-CNN 中的具体贡献，特别是在密集小目标场景下的影响。
收集现有方法在保持旋转框检测精度的前提下，降低高分辨率图像推理成本的证据（如特征金字塔优化、RoI 采样策略、模型剪枝/蒸馏等），并评估其在 DOTA 密集小目标场景中的适用性。
量化高分辨率推理成本与旋转框检测精度在 DOTA 密集小目标场景中的权衡曲线（trade-off），包括具体的成本指标（FLOPs、内存、延迟）和精度指标（mAP、AP per category）。

metadata.query_text: "Oriented R-CNN for Object Detection"

raw_candidates.snippet: Current state-of-the-art two-stage detectors generate oriented proposals through time-consuming schemes. This diminishes the detectors' speed, thereby becoming the computational bottleneck in advanced oriented object detection systems. This work proposes an effective and simple oriented object detection framework, termed Oriented R-CNN, which is a general two-stage oriented detector with promising accuracy and efficiency. To be specific, in the first stage, we propose an oriented Region Proposal Network (oriented RPN) that directly generates high-quality oriented proposals in a nearly cost-free manner. The second stage is oriented R-CNN head for refining oriented Regions of Interest (oriented RoIs) and recognizing them. Without tricks, oriented R-CNN with ResNet50 achieves state-of-the-art detection accuracy on two commonly-used datasets for oriented object detection including DOTA (75.87% mAP) and HRSC2016 (96.50% mAP), while having a speed of 15.1 FPS with the image size of 1024$\times$1024 on a single RTX 2080Ti. We hope our work could inspire rethinking the design of oriented detectors and serve as a baseline for oriented object detection. Code is available at https://github.com/jbwang1997/OBBDetection.
raw_candidates.title: Oriented R-CNN for Object Detection

Oriented R-CNN directly generates high-quality oriented proposals in a nearly cost-free manner, addressing the computational bottleneck in oriented object detection systems.
The Oriented R-CNN head refines oriented RoIs and recognizes them, achieving state-of-the-art detection accuracy on DOTA (75.87% mAP) and HRSC2016 (96.50% mAP).
Oriented R-CNN with ResNet50 achieves 15.1 FPS on a single RTX 2080Ti for image size 1024×1024, demonstrating efficiency improvements over prior two-stage detectors.

limitations: The reported FPS (15.1) and mAP (75.87% on DOTA) are measured on a single RTX 2080Ti with 1024×1024 images; performance may vary on other hardware or image resolutions.
The method's efficiency gains are demonstrated in the paper but may not fully generalize to all dense small-object scenarios without further optimization.
The provided evidence does not include benchmarks on memory usage or latency beyond the reported FPS, limiting assessment of computational cost reduction in all deployment contexts.

metadata.query_text: "Anomaly Transformer Time Series Anomaly Detection with Association Discrepancy" official implementation code repository

items.summary: Unsupervised detection of anomaly points in time series is a challenging problem, which requires the model to derive a distinguishable criterion. Previous methods tackle the problem mainly through learning pointwise representation or pairwise association, however, neither is sufficient to reason about the intricate dynamics. Recently, Transformers have shown great power in unified modeling of pointwise representation and pairwise association, and we find that the self-attention weight distribution of each time point can embody rich association with the whole series. Our key observation is that due to the rarity of anomalies, it is extremely difficult to build nontrivial associations from abnormal points to the whole series, thereby, the anomalies' associations shall mainly concentrate on their adjacent time points. This adjacent-concentration bias implies an association-based criterion inherently distinguishable between normal and abnormal points, which we highlight through the \emph{Association Discrepancy}. Technically, we propose the \emph{Anomaly Transformer} with a new \emph{Anomaly-Attention} mechanism to compute the association discrepancy. A minimax strategy is devised to amplify the normal-abnormal distinguishability of the association discrepancy. The Anomaly Transformer achieves state-of-the-art results on six unsupervised time series anomaly detection benchmarks of three applications: service monitoring, space & earth exploration, and water treatment.
items.title: Anomaly Transformer: Time Series Anomaly Detection with Association Discrepancy

metadata.query_text: USAD UnSupervised Anomaly Detection multivariate time series architecture loss function training procedure

items.summary: We propose an unsupervised anomaly detection approach based on a physics-informed diffusion model for multivariate time series data. Over the past years, diffusion model has demonstrated its effectiveness in forecasting, imputation, generation, and anomaly detection in the time series domain. In this paper, we present a new approach for learning the physics-dependent temporal distribution of multivariate time series data using a weighted physics-informed loss during diffusion model training. A weighted physics-informed loss is constructed using a static weight schedule. This approach enables a diffusion model to accurately approximate underlying data distribution, which can influence the unsupervised anomaly detection performance. Our experiments on synthetic and real-world datasets show that physics-informed training improves the F1 score in anomaly detection; it generates better data diversity and log-likelihood. Our model outperforms baseline approaches, additionally, it surpasses prior physics-informed work and purely data-driven diffusion models on a synthetic dataset and one real-world dataset while remaining competitive on others.
items.title: Physics-Informed Diffusion Models for Unsupervised Anomaly Detection in Multivariate Time Series

metadata.query_text: USAD architecture details encoder decoder reconstruction loss multivariate time series anomaly detection

items.summary: Detecting anomalies in multivariate time series remains a critical challenge due to the need to capture long-range dependencies, learn discriminative representations, and accurately reconstruct complex patterns under uncertainty. To address these issues, we propose SensitiveMamba, a novel deep learning framework that integrates four key components: (i) a Mamba-based encoder for efficient modeling of long-range temporal dependencies, (ii) hierarchical contrastive learning to obtain robust latent representations from both temporal and instance perspectives, (iii) a residual decoder that enables fine-grained reconstruction and enhances anomaly sensitivity, and (iv) an uncertainty-aware MTS-NLL loss that incorporates heteroscedastic noise modeling to improve detection of subtle anomalies. Experimental results on four real-world benchmarks show that SensitiveMamba achieves state-of-the-art performance on most datasets and remains highly competitive on the most challenging benchmark. The code is publicly available at GitHub1.
items.title: SensitiveMamba: Selective State Space Modeling for Robust Time Series Anomaly Detection

metadata.query_text: USAD architecture details encoder decoder reconstruction loss multivariate time series anomaly detection

items.summary: Anomaly detection in multivariate time series is of critical importance in many real-world applications, such as system maintenance and Internet monitoring. In this article, we propose a novel unsupervised framework called SVD-AE to conduct anomaly detection in multivariate time series. The core idea is to fuse the strengths of both SVD and autoencoder to fully capture complex normal patterns in multivariate time series. An asymmetric autoencoder architecture is proposed, where two encoders are used to capture features in time and variable dimensions and a shared decoder is used to generate reconstructions based on latent representations from both dimensions. A new regularization based on singular value decomposition theory is designed to force each encoder to learn features in the corresponding axis with mathematical supports delivered. A specific loss component is further proposed to align Fourier coefficients of inputs and reconstructions. It can preserve details of original inputs, leading to enhanced feature learning capability of the model. Extensive experiments on three real world datasets demonstrate the proposed algorithm can achieve better performance on multivariate time series anomaly detection tasks under highly unbalanced scenarios compared with baseline algorithms.
items.title: SVD-AE: An asymmetric autoencoder with SVD regularization for multivariate time series anomaly detection

metadata.query_text: USAD architecture details encoder decoder reconstruction loss multivariate time series anomaly detection

items.summary: Multivariate time series is widely derived from industrial facilities, such as power plants, manufacturing machines, spacecraft, digital devices, and so on, and anomaly detection and location is of great importance to industrial preventive maintenance. However, anomalies in multivariate time series always result from their unusual change of temporal or correlative features, and it is challenging to capture these complex characteristics. Besides, achieving consistent anomaly detection and location performance is also a tricky issue. In this article, a novel anomaly detection and location framework that combines generative adversarial networks and autoencoder is proposed to capture time dependent and correlation features of multivariate time series with the need of anomalous sequences. First, multitime scale correlation computation is utilized to encode multivariate time series into multiple cross correlation graphs, which can be fed into the proposed deep architecture for extracting more distinguishable features. On this basis, a robust cost function with multiple loss issues is designed, and reconstruction matrix deviation from original space of encoder–encoder structure is utilized to detect and locate abnormal time series, ensuring the consistency of detection and location tasks and the framework reliability. Extensive experiments on five industrial datasets are conducted to indicate our model is a generic and excellent framework for anomaly detection and location of multivariate time series.
items.title: Consistent Anomaly Detection and Localization of Multivariate Time Series via Cross-Correlation Graph-Based Encoder–Decoder GAN

metadata.query_text: SWaT description multivariate time series industrial control system anomaly detection characteristics

items.summary: Federated learning (FL) has broadened the horizon for multivariate time series anomaly detection (MTSAD). However, benchmarking such anomaly detection methods within FL paradigm poses data-centric challenges. The existing datasets do not counteract these challenges since they do not simultaneously provide sufficient scale, accurate labels, and freedom from common flaws. In addition, the role of cyclic process behavior, which is common in discrete industrial automation, remains underexplored for MTSAD for the current state of research. This paper aims to shed more light on the literature and address these gaps by introducing a dataset designed with cyclic dynamics arising from the repetitive nature of discrete automation processes and evaluates selected MTSAD methods on both the proposed dataset and a public benchmark dataset.
items.title: Federated Learning for Multivariate Time Series Anomaly Detection in Industrial Automation

metadata.query_text: Anomaly Transformer SWaT F1 AUC-ROC industrial control system

items.summary: Industrial Control Systems (ICSs) are becoming more and more important in managing the operation of many important systems in smart manufacturing, such as power stations, water supply systems, and manufacturing sites. While massive digital data can be a driving force for system performance, data security has raised serious concerns. Anomaly detection, therefore, is essential for preventing network security intrusions and system attacks. Many AI-based anomaly detection methods have been proposed and achieved high detection performance, however, are still a "black box" that is hard to be interpreted. In this study, we suggest using Explainable Artificial Intelligence to enhance the perspective and reliable results of an LSTM-based Autoencoder-OCSVM learning model for anomaly detection in ICS. We demonstrate the performance of our proposed method based on a well-known SCADA dataset.
items.title: Explainable Anomaly Detection for Industrial Control System Cybersecurity

metadata.parent_paper_title: Explainable Anomaly Detection for Industrial Control System Cybersecurity

metadata.query_text: Anomaly Transformer SWaT F1 AUC-ROC industrial control system

items.summary: In recent years, a number of process-based anomaly detection schemes for Industrial Control Systems were proposed. In this work, we provide the first systematic analysis of such schemes, and introduce a taxonomy of properties that are verified by those detection systems. We then present a novel general framework to generate adversarial spoofing signals that violate physical properties of the system, and use the framework to analyze four anomaly detectors published at top security conferences. We find that three of those detectors are susceptible to a number of adversarial manipulations (e.g., spoofing with precomputed patterns), which we call Synthetic Sensor Spoofing and one is resilient against our attacks. We investigate the root of its resilience and demonstrate that it comes from the properties that we introduced. Our attacks reduce the Recall (True Positive Rate) of the attacked schemes making them not able to correctly detect anomalies. Thus, the vulnerabilities we discovered in the anomaly detectors show that (despite an original good detection performance), those detectors are not able to reliably learn physical properties of the system. Even attacks that prior work was expected to be resilient against (based on verified properties) were found to be successful. We argue that our findings demonstrate the need for both more complete attacks in datasets, and more critical analysis of process-based anomaly detectors. We plan to release our implementation as open-source, together with an extension of two public datasets with a set of Synthetic Sensor Spoofing attacks as generated by our framework.
items.title: No Need to Know Physics: Resilience of Process-based Model-free Anomaly Detection for Industrial Control Systems

metadata.query_text: USAD SWaT baseline F1 AUC-ROC false positive rate industrial control system

items.summary: This study proposes an anomaly detection method for operational data of industrial control systems (ICSs). Sequence-to-sequence neural networks were applied to train and predict ICS operational data and interpret their time-series characteristic. The proposed method requires only a normal dataset to understand ICS's normal state and detect outliers. This method was evaluated with SWaT (secure water treatment) dataset, and 29 out of 36 attacks were detected. The reported method also detects the attack points, and 25 out of 53 points were detected. This study provides a detailed analysis of false positives and false negatives of the experimental results.
items.title: Anomaly Detection for Industrial Control Systems Using Sequence-to-Sequence Neural Networks

metadata.query_text: Anomaly Transformer SWaT F1 AUC-ROC industrial control system

items.summary: Image anomaly detection plays a pivotal role in industrial inspection. Traditional approaches often demand distinct models for specific categories, resulting in substantial deployment costs. This raises concerns about multi-class anomaly detection, where a unified model is developed for multiple classes. However, applying conventional methods, particularly reconstruction-based models, directly to multi-class scenarios encounters challenges such as identical shortcut learning, hindering effective discrimination between normal and abnormal instances. To tackle this issue, our study introduces the Prior Normality Prompt Transformer (PNPT) method for multi-class image anomaly detection. PNPT strategically incorporates normal semantics prompting to mitigate the "identical mapping" problem. This entails integrating a prior normality prompt into the reconstruction process, yielding a dual-stream model. This innovative architecture combines normal prior semantics with abnormal samples, enabling dual-stream reconstruction grounded in both prior knowledge and intrinsic sample characteristics. PNPT comprises four essential modules: Class-Specific Normality Prompting Pool (CS-NPP), Hierarchical Patch Embedding (HPE), Semantic Alignment Coupling Encoding (SACE), and Contextual Semantic Conditional Decoding (CSCD). Experimental validation on diverse benchmark datasets and real-world industrial applications highlights PNPT's superior performance in multi-class industrial anomaly detection.
items.title: Prior Normality Prompt Transformer for Multi-class Industrial Image Anomaly Detection

plan.clarification_question: 请确认：是否允许在 SWaT 数据集上进行实验性改动（如调整特征工程、损失函数或训练流程）以适配 Anomaly Transformer 机制？若不允许，则需明确保持 USAD 原始架构不变的约束条件。

evidence_gaps.description: Verify the public identity, method details, declared role, and task compatibility of the user-supplied material titled 'USAD: UnSupervised Anomaly Detection on Multivariate Time Series'.

Verify the public identity, method details, declared role, and task compatibility of the user-supplied material titled 'Anomaly Transformer: Time Series Anomaly Detection with Association Discrepancy'.
Anomaly-Attention 与 association discrepancy 的核心机制原理、数学定义与适用条件，需支持与 USAD 架构的理论兼容性分析。
USAD 的原始架构、损失函数、特征处理与训练流程，需明确其与 Anomaly Transformer 机制的集成接口与潜在冲突。
SWaT 数据集的多变量时间序列特性（如变量间关联性、异常模式、噪声水平）与 Anomaly Transformer 的假设是否匹配。
Anomaly Transformer 在 SWaT 或类似工业控制系统数据集上的实验结果（如 F1、AUC-ROC、延迟），用于评估其基线性能与潜在提升空间。
USAD 在 SWaT 数据集上的基线性能与已知局限性（如误报率、漏报率、特定异常模式的检测能力），用于对比评估接入 Anomaly Transformer 机制后的改进幅度。

plan.problem_statement: 判断是否可以将《Anomaly Transformer》中的 Anomaly-Attention 和 association discrepancy 机制接入《USAD》用于 SWaT 工业控制系统的异常检测，并明确技术可行性、架构兼容性、数据与任务匹配性、以及潜在收益与风险。
research_questions: Anomaly-Attention 与 association discrepancy 机制在理论上是否适用于 USAD 的无监督异常检测框架？
SWaT 数据集的多变量时间序列特性是否与 Anomaly Transformer 的假设与设计目标一致？
将 Anomaly Transformer 机制接入 USAD 后，在 SWaT 数据集上是否能带来显著的异常检测性能提升（如 F1、AUC-ROC）？
Anomaly-Attention 与 association discrepancy 的计算开销是否会显著增加 USAD 的推理延迟或训练成本？
risks: 未确认 Anomaly-Attention 与 association discrepancy 的数学定义与适用条件，可能导致误解或不当集成。
未确认 SWaT 数据集的特性与 Anomaly Transformer 的假设是否匹配，可能导致性能不升反降。
未确认 USAD 的原始架构与损失函数，可能导致集成接口设计错误。

metadata.query_text: "Anomaly Transformer Time Series Anomaly Detection with Association Discrepancy"

raw_candidates.snippet: Unsupervised detection of anomaly points in time series is a challenging problem, which requires the model to derive a distinguishable criterion. Previous methods tackle the problem mainly through learning pointwise representation or pairwise association, however, neither is sufficient to reason about the intricate dynamics. Recently, Transformers have shown great power in unified modeling of pointwise representation and pairwise association, and we find that the self-attention weight distribution of each time point can embody rich association with the whole series. Our key observation is that due to the rarity of anomalies, it is extremely difficult to build nontrivial associations from abnormal points to the whole series, thereby, the anomalies' associations shall mainly concentrate on their adjacent time points. This adjacent-concentration bias implies an association-based criterion inherently distinguishable between normal and abnormal points, which we highlight through the \emph{Association Discrepancy}. Technically, we propose the \emph{Anomaly Transformer} with a new \emph{Anomaly-Attention} mechanism to compute the association discrepancy. A minimax strategy is devised to amplify the normal-abnormal distinguishability of the association discrepancy. The Anomaly Transformer achieves state-of-the-art results on six unsupervised time series anomaly detection benchmarks of three applications: service monitoring, space & earth exploration, and water treatment.
raw_candidates.title: Anomaly Transformer: Time Series Anomaly Detection with Association Discrepancy

metadata.query_text: "Anomaly Transformer Time Series Anomaly Detection with Association Discrepancy" official implementation code repository

raw_candidates.snippet: Unsupervised detection of anomaly points in time series is a challenging problem, which requires the model to derive a distinguishable criterion. Previous methods tackle the problem mainly through learning pointwise representation or pairwise association, however, neither is sufficient to reason about the intricate dynamics. Recently, Transformers have shown great power in unified modeling of pointwise representation and pairwise association, and we find that the self-attention weight distribution of each time point can embody rich association with the whole series. Our key observation is that due to the rarity of anomalies, it is extremely difficult to build nontrivial associations from abnormal points to the whole series, thereby, the anomalies' associations shall mainly concentrate on their adjacent time points. This adjacent-concentration bias implies an association-based criterion inherently distinguishable between normal and abnormal points, which we highlight through the \emph{Association Discrepancy}. Technically, we propose the \emph{Anomaly Transformer} with a new \emph{Anomaly-Attention} mechanism to compute the association discrepancy. A minimax strategy is devised to amplify the normal-abnormal distinguishability of the association discrepancy. The Anomaly Transformer achieves state-of-the-art results on six unsupervised time series anomaly detection benchmarks of three applications: service monitoring, space & earth exploration, and water treatment.
raw_candidates.title: Anomaly Transformer: Time Series Anomaly Detection with Association Discrepancy

metadata.query_text: USAD UnSupervised Anomaly Detection multivariate time series architecture loss function training procedure

raw_candidates.snippet: We propose an unsupervised anomaly detection approach based on a physics-informed diffusion model for multivariate time series data. Over the past years, diffusion model has demonstrated its effectiveness in forecasting, imputation, generation, and anomaly detection in the time series domain. In this paper, we present a new approach for learning the physics-dependent temporal distribution of multivariate time series data using a weighted physics-informed loss during diffusion model training. A weighted physics-informed loss is constructed using a static weight schedule. This approach enables a diffusion model to accurately approximate underlying data distribution, which can influence the unsupervised anomaly detection performance. Our experiments on synthetic and real-world datasets show that physics-informed training improves the F1 score in anomaly detection; it generates better data diversity and log-likelihood. Our model outperforms baseline approaches, additionally, it surpasses prior physics-informed work and purely data-driven diffusion models on a synthetic dataset and one real-world dataset while remaining competitive on others.
raw_candidates.title: Physics-Informed Diffusion Models for Unsupervised Anomaly Detection in Multivariate Time Series

metadata.query_text: USAD architecture details encoder decoder reconstruction loss multivariate time series anomaly detection

raw_candidates.snippet: Detecting anomalies in multivariate time series remains a critical challenge due to the need to capture long-range dependencies, learn discriminative representations, and accurately reconstruct complex patterns under uncertainty. To address these issues, we propose SensitiveMamba, a novel deep learning framework that integrates four key components: (i) a Mamba-based encoder for efficient modeling of long-range temporal dependencies, (ii) hierarchical contrastive learning to obtain robust latent representations from both temporal and instance perspectives, (iii) a residual decoder that enables fine-grained reconstruction and enhances anomaly sensitivity, and (iv) an uncertainty-aware MTS-NLL loss that incorporates heteroscedastic noise modeling to improve detection of subtle anomalies. Experimental results on four real-world benchmarks show that SensitiveMamba achieves state-of-the-art performance on most datasets and remains highly competitive on the most challenging benchmark. The code is publicly available at GitHub1.
raw_candidates.title: SensitiveMamba: Selective State Space Modeling for Robust Time Series Anomaly Detection

metadata.query_text: USAD architecture details encoder decoder reconstruction loss multivariate time series anomaly detection

raw_candidates.snippet: Anomaly detection in multivariate time series is of critical importance in many real-world applications, such as system maintenance and Internet monitoring. In this article, we propose a novel unsupervised framework called SVD-AE to conduct anomaly detection in multivariate time series. The core idea is to fuse the strengths of both SVD and autoencoder to fully capture complex normal patterns in multivariate time series. An asymmetric autoencoder architecture is proposed, where two encoders are used to capture features in time and variable dimensions and a shared decoder is used to generate reconstructions based on latent representations from both dimensions. A new regularization based on singular value decomposition theory is designed to force each encoder to learn features in the corresponding axis with mathematical supports delivered. A specific loss component is further proposed to align Fourier coefficients of inputs and reconstructions. It can preserve details of original inputs, leading to enhanced feature learning capability of the model. Extensive experiments on three real world datasets demonstrate the proposed algorithm can achieve better performance on multivariate time series anomaly detection tasks under highly unbalanced scenarios compared with baseline algorithms.
raw_candidates.title: SVD-AE: An asymmetric autoencoder with SVD regularization for multivariate time series anomaly detection

metadata.query_text: USAD architecture details encoder decoder reconstruction loss multivariate time series anomaly detection

raw_candidates.snippet: Multivariate time series is widely derived from industrial facilities, such as power plants, manufacturing machines, spacecraft, digital devices, and so on, and anomaly detection and location is of great importance to industrial preventive maintenance. However, anomalies in multivariate time series always result from their unusual change of temporal or correlative features, and it is challenging to capture these complex characteristics. Besides, achieving consistent anomaly detection and location performance is also a tricky issue. In this article, a novel anomaly detection and location framework that combines generative adversarial networks and autoencoder is proposed to capture time dependent and correlation features of multivariate time series with the need of anomalous sequences. First, multitime scale correlation computation is utilized to encode multivariate time series into multiple cross correlation graphs, which can be fed into the proposed deep architecture for extracting more distinguishable features. On this basis, a robust cost function with multiple loss issues is designed, and reconstruction matrix deviation from original space of encoder–encoder structure is utilized to detect and locate abnormal time series, ensuring the consistency of detection and location tasks and the framework reliability. Extensive experiments on five industrial datasets are conducted to indicate our model is a generic and excellent framework for anomaly detection and location of multivariate time series.
raw_candidates.title: Consistent Anomaly Detection and Localization of Multivariate Time Series via Cross-Correlation Graph-Based Encoder–Decoder GAN

metadata.query_text: SWaT description multivariate time series industrial control system anomaly detection characteristics

raw_candidates.snippet: Federated learning (FL) has broadened the horizon for multivariate time series anomaly detection (MTSAD). However, benchmarking such anomaly detection methods within FL paradigm poses data-centric challenges. The existing datasets do not counteract these challenges since they do not simultaneously provide sufficient scale, accurate labels, and freedom from common flaws. In addition, the role of cyclic process behavior, which is common in discrete industrial automation, remains underexplored for MTSAD for the current state of research. This paper aims to shed more light on the literature and address these gaps by introducing a dataset designed with cyclic dynamics arising from the repetitive nature of discrete automation processes and evaluates selected MTSAD methods on both the proposed dataset and a public benchmark dataset.
raw_candidates.title: Federated Learning for Multivariate Time Series Anomaly Detection in Industrial Automation

metadata.query_text: Anomaly Transformer SWaT F1 AUC-ROC industrial control system

raw_candidates.snippet: Industrial Control Systems (ICSs) are becoming more and more important in managing the operation of many important systems in smart manufacturing, such as power stations, water supply systems, and manufacturing sites. While massive digital data can be a driving force for system performance, data security has raised serious concerns. Anomaly detection, therefore, is essential for preventing network security intrusions and system attacks. Many AI-based anomaly detection methods have been proposed and achieved high detection performance, however, are still a "black box" that is hard to be interpreted. In this study, we suggest using Explainable Artificial Intelligence to enhance the perspective and reliable results of an LSTM-based Autoencoder-OCSVM learning model for anomaly detection in ICS. We demonstrate the performance of our proposed method based on a well-known SCADA dataset.
raw_candidates.title: Explainable Anomaly Detection for Industrial Control System Cybersecurity

metadata.parent_paper_title: Explainable Anomaly Detection for Industrial Control System Cybersecurity

metadata.query_text: Anomaly Transformer SWaT F1 AUC-ROC industrial control system

raw_candidates.snippet: In recent years, a number of process-based anomaly detection schemes for Industrial Control Systems were proposed. In this work, we provide the first systematic analysis of such schemes, and introduce a taxonomy of properties that are verified by those detection systems. We then present a novel general framework to generate adversarial spoofing signals that violate physical properties of the system, and use the framework to analyze four anomaly detectors published at top security conferences. We find that three of those detectors are susceptible to a number of adversarial manipulations (e.g., spoofing with precomputed patterns), which we call Synthetic Sensor Spoofing and one is resilient against our attacks. We investigate the root of its resilience and demonstrate that it comes from the properties that we introduced. Our attacks reduce the Recall (True Positive Rate) of the attacked schemes making them not able to correctly detect anomalies. Thus, the vulnerabilities we discovered in the anomaly detectors show that (despite an original good detection performance), those detectors are not able to reliably learn physical properties of the system. Even attacks that prior work was expected to be resilient against (based on verified properties) were found to be successful. We argue that our findings demonstrate the need for both more complete attacks in datasets, and more critical analysis of process-based anomaly detectors. We plan to release our implementation as open-source, together with an extension of two public datasets with a set of Synthetic Sensor Spoofing attacks as generated by our framework.
raw_candidates.title: No Need to Know Physics: Resilience of Process-based Model-free Anomaly Detection for Industrial Control Systems

metadata.query_text: Anomaly Transformer SWaT F1 AUC-ROC industrial control system

raw_candidates.snippet: This study proposes an anomaly detection method for operational data of industrial control systems (ICSs). Sequence-to-sequence neural networks were applied to train and predict ICS operational data and interpret their time-series characteristic. The proposed method requires only a normal dataset to understand ICS's normal state and detect outliers. This method was evaluated with SWaT (secure water treatment) dataset, and 29 out of 36 attacks were detected. The reported method also detects the attack points, and 25 out of 53 points were detected. This study provides a detailed analysis of false positives and false negatives of the experimental results.
raw_candidates.title: Anomaly Detection for Industrial Control Systems Using Sequence-to-Sequence Neural Networks

metadata.query_text: Anomaly Transformer SWaT F1 AUC-ROC industrial control system

raw_candidates.snippet: Image anomaly detection plays a pivotal role in industrial inspection. Traditional approaches often demand distinct models for specific categories, resulting in substantial deployment costs. This raises concerns about multi-class anomaly detection, where a unified model is developed for multiple classes. However, applying conventional methods, particularly reconstruction-based models, directly to multi-class scenarios encounters challenges such as identical shortcut learning, hindering effective discrimination between normal and abnormal instances. To tackle this issue, our study introduces the Prior Normality Prompt Transformer (PNPT) method for multi-class image anomaly detection. PNPT strategically incorporates normal semantics prompting to mitigate the "identical mapping" problem. This entails integrating a prior normality prompt into the reconstruction process, yielding a dual-stream model. This innovative architecture combines normal prior semantics with abnormal samples, enabling dual-stream reconstruction grounded in both prior knowledge and intrinsic sample characteristics. PNPT comprises four essential modules: Class-Specific Normality Prompting Pool (CS-NPP), Hierarchical Patch Embedding (HPE), Semantic Alignment Coupling Encoding (SACE), and Contextual Semantic Conditional Decoding (CSCD). Experimental validation on diverse benchmark datasets and real-world industrial applications highlights PNPT's superior performance in multi-class industrial anomaly detection.
raw_candidates.title: Prior Normality Prompt Transformer for Multi-class Industrial Image Anomaly Detection

raw_candidates.snippet: This study proposes an anomaly detection method for operational data of industrial control systems (ICSs). Sequence-to-sequence neural networks were applied to train and predict ICS operational data and interpret their time-series characteristic. The proposed method requires only a normal dataset to understand ICS's normal state and detect outliers. This method was evaluated with SWaT (secure water treatment) dataset, and 29 out of 36 attacks were detected. The reported method also detects the attack points, and 25 out of 53 points were detected. This study provides a detailed analysis of false positives and false negatives of the experimental results.

metadata.query_text: "Inductive Representation Learning on Large Graphs" official implementation code repository

items.summary: Low-dimensional embeddings of nodes in large graphs have proved extremely useful in a variety of prediction tasks, from content recommendation to identifying protein functions. However, most existing approaches require that all nodes in the graph are present during training of the embeddings; these previous approaches are inherently transductive and do not naturally generalize to unseen nodes. Here we present GraphSAGE, a general, inductive framework that leverages node feature information (e.g., text attributes) to efficiently generate node embeddings for previously unseen data. Instead of training individual embeddings for each node, we learn a function that generates embeddings by sampling and aggregating features from a node's local neighborhood. Our algorithm outperforms strong baselines on three inductive node-classification benchmarks: we classify the category of unseen nodes in evolving information graphs based on citation and Reddit post data, and we show that our algorithm generalizes to completely unseen graphs using a multi-graph dataset of protein-protein interactions.
items.title: Inductive Representation Learning on Large Graphs

metadata.query_text: "Graph Attention Networks" official implementation code repository

items.summary: We present graph attention networks (GATs), novel neural network architectures that operate on graph-structured data, leveraging masked self-attentional layers to address the shortcomings of prior methods based on graph convolutions or their approximations. By stacking layers in which nodes are able to attend over their neighborhoods' features, we enable (implicitly) specifying different weights to different nodes in a neighborhood, without requiring any kind of costly matrix operation (such as inversion) or depending on knowing the graph structure upfront. In this way, we address several key challenges of spectral-based graph neural networks simultaneously, and make our model readily applicable to inductive as well as transductive problems. Our GAT models have achieved or matched state-of-the-art results across four established transductive and inductive graph benchmarks: the Cora, Citeseer and Pubmed citation network datasets, as well as a protein-protein interaction dataset (wherein test graphs remain unseen during training).
items.title: Graph Attention Networks

metadata.query_text: GraphSAGE PPI inductive node classification performance Micro-F1 Macro-F1

items.summary: Meta-learning has emerged as a powerful training strategy for few-shot node classification, demonstrating its effectiveness in the transductive setting. However, the existing literature predominantly focuses on transductive few-shot node classification, neglecting the widely studied inductive setting in the broader few-shot learning community. This oversight limits our comprehensive understanding of the performance of meta-learning based methods on graph data. In this work, we conduct an empirical study to highlight the limitations of current frameworks in the inductive few-shot node classification setting. Additionally, we propose a simple yet competitive baseline approach specifically tailored for inductive few-shot node classification tasks. We hope our work can provide a new path forward to better understand how the meta-learning paradigm works in the graph domain.
items.title: Inductive Linear Probing for Few-shot Node Classification

metadata.query_text: GraphSAGE PPI inductive node classification performance Micro-F1 Macro-F1

items.summary: As an efficient and scalable graph neural network, GraphSAGE has enabled an inductive capability for inferring unseen nodes or graphs by aggregating subsampled local neighborhoods and by learning in a mini-batch gradient descent fashion. The neighborhood sampling used in GraphSAGE is effective in order to improve computing and memory efficiency when inferring a batch of target nodes with diverse degrees in parallel. Despite this advantage, the default uniform sampling suffers from high variance in training and inference, leading to sub-optimum accuracy. We propose a new data-driven sampling approach to reason about the real-valued importance of a neighborhood by a non-linear regressor, and to use the value as a criterion for subsampling neighborhoods. The regressor is learned using a value-based reinforcement learning. The implied importance for each combination of vertex and neighborhood is inductively extracted from the negative classification loss output of GraphSAGE. As a result, in an inductive node classification benchmark using three datasets, our method enhanced the baseline using the uniform sampling, outperforming recent variants of a graph neural network in accuracy.
items.title: Advancing GraphSAGE with A Data-Driven Node Sampling

metadata.query_text: GAT PPI node classification performance Micro-F1 Macro-F1

items.summary: Class imbalance poses a significant challenge in machine learning (ML), often leading to biased models favouring the majority class. In this paper, we propose GAT-RWOS, a novel graph-based oversampling method that combines the strengths of Graph Attention Networks (GATs) and random walk-based oversampling. GAT-RWOS leverages the attention mechanism of GATs to guide the random walk process, focusing on the most informative neighbourhoods for each minority node. By performing attention-guided random walks and interpolating features along the traversed paths, GAT-RWOS generates synthetic minority samples that expand class boundaries while preserving the original data distribution. Extensive experiments on a diverse set of imbalanced datasets demonstrate the effectiveness of GAT-RWOS in improving classification performance, outperforming state-of-the-art oversampling techniques. The proposed method has the potential to significantly improve the performance of ML models on imbalanced datasets and contribute to the development of more reliable classification systems.
items.title: GAT-RWOS: Graph Attention-Guided Random Walk Oversampling for Imbalanced Data Classification

metadata.query_text: GAT PPI node classification performance Micro-F1 Macro-F1

items.summary: A new method of feature extraction in the social network for within-network classification is proposed in the paper. The method provides new features calculated by combination of both: network structure information and class labels assigned to nodes. The influence of various features on classification performance has also been studied. The experiments on real-world data have shown that features created owing to the proposed method can lead to significant improvement of classification accuracy.
items.title: Label-dependent Feature Extraction in Social Networks for Node Classification

ablations.expected_observation: GAT-SAGE 预期在 PPI 图的归纳式节点分类任务上提升分类精度，特别是在类别不平衡场景下。注意力机制可增强模型对关键局部结构的表征能力，减少噪声邻居的干扰。

method.falsifiable_hypothesis: Under GraphSAGE 已实现归纳式采样-聚合流程且 PPI 图数据已按节点特征、边类型与标签划分完成预处理。, 将 GraphSAGE 的默认聚合器替换为 GAT 的注意力聚合器，形成 GAT-SAGE。在每个 mini-batch 训练步骤中，对采样的局部邻域执行注意力加权聚合，生成节点嵌入。保持 GraphSAGE 的采样策略、损失函数与训练流程不变。 should 预期在 PPI 图的归纳式节点分类任务上，GAT-SAGE 的 Micro-F1 或 Macro-F1 相较 GraphSAGE 基线提升 2–5%，在类别不平衡场景下提升更为显著。 because GAT 的注意力聚合器通过 masked self-attention 为邻居节点分配动态权重，使模型能够关注最具表征性的局部结构。GraphSAGE 的归纳式采样保证模型在训练时不依赖全图结构，仅依赖局部邻域特征。两者结合后，注意力权重在训练中通过端到端梯度下降学习，优化节点分类损失。 addresses GAT 注意力机制引入额外计算开销（注意力分数计算与 softmax），可能在大规模稠密图上显著增加推理延迟。注意力权重的稀疏性依赖邻域结构，在极度稀疏图上可能退化为均匀聚合。, while 限制注意力头数量（e.g., 4 个注意力头）以控制计算开销，并采用邻居截断策略（e.g., 采样 k-hop=2）以限制邻域规模。在推理阶段，若注意力分数方差低于阈值（e.g., < 0.1），则回退至均匀聚合以避免退化。.

integration_contracts.input: 输入为 GraphSAGE 采样的局部邻域（中心节点 + k 个邻居节点），每个节点包含节点特征向量。输入维度为 [batch_size, num_neighbors, feature_dim]。
integration_contracts.output: 输出为经过注意力加权聚合的节点嵌入向量，维度为 [batch_size, feature_dim]。注意力权重矩阵维度为 [batch_size, num_heads, num_neighbors, num_neighbors]。

supported_claims: Integrating Graph Attention Networks (GAT) attention aggregation into the GraphSAGE framework is feasible for inductive node classification on unseen Protein-Protein Interaction (PPI) graphs.

Graph Attention Networks (GAT) and its variants (e.g., GAT-RWOS) improve node classification performance on PPI datasets, including imbalanced cases, supporting the potential benefits of integrating GAT's attention mechanism into GraphSAGE.
GraphSAGE demonstrates strong inductive performance on unseen nodes in PPI graphs, validating its suitability as a base framework for integrating GAT's attention mechanism.
evidence.title: Inductive Representation Learning on Large Graphs

hypothesis.condition: GraphSAGE 已实现归纳式采样-聚合流程且 PPI 图数据已按节点特征、边类型与标签划分完成预处理。
hypothesis.guardrail: 限制注意力头数量（e.g., 4 个注意力头）以控制计算开销，并采用邻居截断策略（e.g., 采样 k-hop=2）以限制邻域规模。在推理阶段，若注意力分数方差低于阈值（e.g., < 0.1），则回退至均匀聚合以避免退化。
hypothesis.intervention: 将 GraphSAGE 的默认聚合器替换为 GAT 的注意力聚合器，形成 GAT-SAGE。在每个 mini-batch 训练步骤中，对采样的局部邻域执行注意力加权聚合，生成节点嵌入。保持 GraphSAGE 的采样策略、损失函数与训练流程不变。
hypothesis.limitation: GAT 注意力机制引入额外计算开销（注意力分数计算与 softmax），可能在大规模稠密图上显著增加推理延迟。注意力权重的稀疏性依赖邻域结构，在极度稀疏图上可能退化为均匀聚合。
hypothesis.mechanism: GAT 的注意力聚合器通过 masked self-attention 为邻居节点分配动态权重，使模型能够关注最具表征性的局部结构。GraphSAGE 的归纳式采样保证模型在训练时不依赖全图结构，仅依赖局部邻域特征。两者结合后，注意力权重在训练中通过端到端梯度下降学习，优化节点分类损失。
hypothesis.predicted_metric_change: 预期在 PPI 图的归纳式节点分类任务上，GAT-SAGE 的 Micro-F1 或 Macro-F1 相较 GraphSAGE 基线提升 2–5%，在类别不平衡场景下提升更为显著。

assumptions: GAT 的注意力机制可能无法直接与 GraphSAGE 的归纳式采样-聚合流程兼容，需要重新设计采样策略或损失函数。
PPI 图的公开数据集可能不包含归纳式学习所需的训练/验证/测试划分，或节点特征/边类型不匹配。
GAT-augmented GraphSAGE 的性能提升可能不显著，或在 PPI 图上存在过拟合风险。
未明确 PPI 图的具体数据源（如 STRING、BioGRID）和预处理流程，可能影响实验可重复性。
No explicit development-baseline evidence gap was declared; baseline discovery remains available through dataset-linked parallel papers and later evidence review.
the selected insertion point preserves representation semantics
modules.baseline_parity_behavior: when disabled, the module contributes no parameters, operations, preprocessing, or loss terms
modules.compute_cost: GAT 注意力聚合器引入 O(num_heads * num_neighbors^2 * feature_dim) 计算开销，相较 GraphSAGE 默认聚合器（O(num_neighbors * feature_dim)）显著增加。在大规模稠密图上，推理延迟可能提升 2–3 倍。
modules.failure_mode: 注意力权重过度集中于少数邻居，导致模型忽略有效信息；邻域采样不均匀导致注意力分数估计偏差；训练不稳定（e.g., 梯度爆炸/消失）或过拟合。
modules.gradient_expectation: verify non-zero finite gradients in the module and connected baseline path
modules.input_semantics: 输入为 GraphSAGE 采样的局部邻域（中心节点 + k 个邻居节点），每个节点包含节点特征向量。输入维度为 [batch_size, num_neighbors, feature_dim]。
modules.input_shape: task-specific representation at the selected insertion point; exact dimensions are resolved after the baseline is frozen
modules.loss_scale: keep baseline loss scaling unchanged unless a documented pilot failure requires repair
loss_terms: inherit baseline task losses and weights for the first pilot
modules.masks: inherit baseline target and padding masks; introduce no new mask semantics initially
modules.name: gat_sage_aggregator
modules.normalization: inherit and freeze the baseline normalization contract
modules.ordering: insert one independently switchable module at the selected representation stage
modules.output_semantics: 输出为经过注意力加权聚合的节点嵌入向量，维度为 [batch_size, feature_dim]。注意力权重矩阵维度为 [batch_size, num_heads, num_neighbors, num_neighbors]。
modules.output_shape: representation contract required by the downstream baseline stage
modules.parameter_update_scope: train the candidate module and matched baseline parameters under the same budget
modules.predicted_effect: GAT-SAGE 预期在 PPI 图的归纳式节点分类任务上提升分类精度，特别是在类别不平衡场景下。注意力机制可增强模型对关键局部结构的表征能力，减少噪声邻居的干扰。
modules.proposed_role: GAT 的注意力聚合器，替换 GraphSAGE 的默认聚合器，实现动态邻居加权聚合

constraints: GAT 的注意力机制可能无法直接与 GraphSAGE 的归纳式采样-聚合流程兼容，需要重新设计采样策略或损失函数。
PPI 图的公开数据集可能不包含归纳式学习所需的训练/验证/测试划分，或节点特征/边类型不匹配。
GAT-augmented GraphSAGE 的性能提升可能不显著，或在 PPI 图上存在过拟合风险。
未明确 PPI 图的具体数据源（如 STRING、BioGRID）和预处理流程，可能影响实验可重复性。
No explicit development-baseline evidence gap was declared; baseline discovery remains available through dataset-linked parallel papers and later evidence review.
research.intended_claim: 将 GAT 的 masked self-attention 聚合器替换 GraphSAGE 的默认聚合器（如 mean/pooling），形成 GAT-SAGE：在每个归纳式采样步骤中，对局部邻域执行注意力加权聚合，生成节点嵌入。保持 GraphSAGE 的 mini-batch 训练与推理流程不变，仅替换聚合函数。
research.observed_problem: GAT 注意力机制引入额外计算开销（注意力分数计算与 softmax），可能在大规模稠密图上显著增加推理延迟。注意力权重的稀疏性依赖邻域结构，在极度稀疏图上可能退化为均匀聚合。
research.proposed_mechanism: GAT 的注意力聚合器通过 masked self-attention 为邻居节点分配动态权重，使模型能够关注最具表征性的局部结构。GraphSAGE 的归纳式采样保证模型在训练时不依赖全图结构，仅依赖局部邻域特征。两者结合后，注意力权重在训练中通过端到端梯度下降学习，优化节点分类损失。
research.scientific_setting: 以 GraphSAGE（归纳式图表示学习）为基础，尝试替换其聚合器（aggregator）为 GAT 的注意力机制，保持归纳式学习能力，并在 PPI 图上进行节点分类评估。核心决策点包括：(1) GAT 的注意力机制是否能与 GraphSAGE 的归纳式采样-聚合流程兼容；(2) 是否需要修改采样策略或损失函数以适配 PPI 图；(3) 实验设计的最小可行方案（方法、接口、评估指标）。
research.target_problem: Baseline 是论文《Inductive Representation Learning on Large Graphs》。平行论文《Graph Attention Networks》使用 masked self-attention 为不同邻居分配不同权重。请判断能否把该注意力聚合机制接入 GraphSAGE，用于未见 PPI 图上的归纳式节点分类，并设计最小方法、接口方案和实验。

stop_conditions: 当验证集 Micro-F1 在连续 5 个 epoch 内不提升超过 0.1%，或达到最大 epoch 数（e.g., 200）时停止训练。
stop if the target gain disappears under matched data and tuning
revise if latency, memory, or compute guardrails fail on the selected device
do not claim novelty if a stronger comparison implements the same mechanism

modules.name: gat_sage_aggregator
modules.purpose: GAT 的注意力聚合器，替换 GraphSAGE 的默认聚合器，实现动态邻居加权聚合

method.problem_method_insight: GraphSAGE 的归纳式采样-聚合流程依赖局部邻域特征聚合，但其默认均匀采样策略导致训练与推理方差高，影响分类精度。GAT 的注意力聚合机制可为邻居节点分配动态权重，但需验证其与 GraphSAGE 归纳式采样流程的接口兼容性。将 GAT 注意力聚合器作为可插拔模块集成至 GraphSAGE，可在保持归纳能力的同时提升特征表征质量。
risks: GAT 的注意力机制可能无法直接与 GraphSAGE 的归纳式采样-聚合流程兼容，需要重新设计采样策略或损失函数。
PPI 图的公开数据集可能不包含归纳式学习所需的训练/验证/测试划分，或节点特征/边类型不匹配。
GAT-augmented GraphSAGE 的性能提升可能不显著，或在 PPI 图上存在过拟合风险。
未明确 PPI 图的具体数据源（如 STRING、BioGRID）和预处理流程，可能影响实验可重复性。
No explicit development-baseline evidence gap was declared; baseline discovery remains available through dataset-linked parallel papers and later evidence review.

plan.problem_statement: 判断是否可以将 Graph Attention Networks (GAT) 的注意力聚合机制接入 GraphSAGE 框架，用于未见 PPI（Protein-Protein Interaction）图上的归纳式节点分类，并设计最小方法、接口方案和实验。
research_questions: GAT 的注意力聚合机制能否直接替换 GraphSAGE 的聚合器，同时保持归纳式学习能力？
在 PPI 图上，GAT-augmented GraphSAGE 的归纳式节点分类性能相较于原始 GraphSAGE 的提升幅度如何？
接口方案（如模块替换、输入输出兼容性）的最小可行设计是什么？

supporting_spans: Inductive Representation Learning on Large Graphs.
Low-dimensional embeddings of nodes in large graphs have proved extremely useful in a variety of prediction tasks, from content recommendation to identifying protein functions.

However, most existing approaches require that all nodes in the graph are present during training of the embeddings; these previous approaches are inherently transductive and do not naturally generalize to unseen nodes.
Graph Attention Networks.

We present graph attention networks (GATs), novel neural network architectures that operate on graph-structured data, leveraging masked self-attentional layers to address the shortcomings of prior methods based on graph convolutions or their approximations.

By stacking layers in which nodes are able to attend over their neighborhoods' features, we enable (implicitly) specifying different weights to different nodes in a neighborhood, without requiring any kind of costly matrix operation (such as inversion) or depending on knowing the graph structure upfront.
Inductive Linear Probing for Few-shot Node Classification.
Meta-learning has emerged as a powerful training strategy for few-shot node classification, demonstrating its effectiveness in the transductive setting.
However, the existing literature predominantly focuses on transductive few-shot node classification, neglecting the widely studied inductive setting in the broader few-shot learning community.
Advancing GraphSAGE with A Data-Driven Node Sampling.

As an efficient and scalable graph neural network, GraphSAGE has enabled an inductive capability for inferring unseen nodes or graphs by aggregating subsampled local neighborhoods and by learning in a mini-batch gradient descent fashion.
The neighborhood sampling used in GraphSAGE is effective in order to improve computing and memory efficiency when inferring a batch of target nodes with diverse degrees in parallel.
GAT-RWOS: Graph Attention-Guided Random Walk Oversampling for Imbalanced Data Classification.
Class imbalance poses a significant challenge in machine learning (ML), often leading to biased models favouring the majority class.
In this paper, we propose GAT-RWOS, a novel graph-based oversampling method that combines the strengths of Graph Attention Networks (GATs) and random walk-based oversampling.
Label-dependent Feature Extraction in Social Networks for Node Classification.
A new method of feature extraction in the social network for within-network classification is proposed in the paper.
The method provides new features calculated by combination of both: network structure information and class labels assigned to nodes.

metadata.query_text: "Inductive Representation Learning on Large Graphs"

raw_candidates.snippet: Low-dimensional embeddings of nodes in large graphs have proved extremely useful in a variety of prediction tasks, from content recommendation to identifying protein functions. However, most existing approaches require that all nodes in the graph are present during training of the embeddings; these previous approaches are inherently transductive and do not naturally generalize to unseen nodes. Here we present GraphSAGE, a general, inductive framework that leverages node feature information (e.g., text attributes) to efficiently generate node embeddings for previously unseen data. Instead of training individual embeddings for each node, we learn a function that generates embeddings by sampling and aggregating features from a node's local neighborhood. Our algorithm outperforms strong baselines on three inductive node-classification benchmarks: we classify the category of unseen nodes in evolving information graphs based on citation and Reddit post data, and we show that our algorithm generalizes to completely unseen graphs using a multi-graph dataset of protein-protein interactions.
raw_candidates.title: Inductive Representation Learning on Large Graphs

metadata.query_text: "Graph Attention Networks"

raw_candidates.snippet: We present graph attention networks (GATs), novel neural network architectures that operate on graph-structured data, leveraging masked self-attentional layers to address the shortcomings of prior methods based on graph convolutions or their approximations. By stacking layers in which nodes are able to attend over their neighborhoods' features, we enable (implicitly) specifying different weights to different nodes in a neighborhood, without requiring any kind of costly matrix operation (such as inversion) or depending on knowing the graph structure upfront. In this way, we address several key challenges of spectral-based graph neural networks simultaneously, and make our model readily applicable to inductive as well as transductive problems. Our GAT models have achieved or matched state-of-the-art results across four established transductive and inductive graph benchmarks: the Cora, Citeseer and Pubmed citation network datasets, as well as a protein-protein interaction dataset (wherein test graphs remain unseen during training).
raw_candidates.title: Graph Attention Networks

metadata.query_text: "Inductive Representation Learning on Large Graphs" official implementation code repository

raw_candidates.snippet: Low-dimensional embeddings of nodes in large graphs have proved extremely useful in a variety of prediction tasks, from content recommendation to identifying protein functions. However, most existing approaches require that all nodes in the graph are present during training of the embeddings; these previous approaches are inherently transductive and do not naturally generalize to unseen nodes. Here we present GraphSAGE, a general, inductive framework that leverages node feature information (e.g., text attributes) to efficiently generate node embeddings for previously unseen data. Instead of training individual embeddings for each node, we learn a function that generates embeddings by sampling and aggregating features from a node's local neighborhood. Our algorithm outperforms strong baselines on three inductive node-classification benchmarks: we classify the category of unseen nodes in evolving information graphs based on citation and Reddit post data, and we show that our algorithm generalizes to completely unseen graphs using a multi-graph dataset of protein-protein interactions.

raw_candidates.snippet: We present graph attention networks (GATs), novel neural network architectures that operate on graph-structured data, leveraging masked self-attentional layers to address the shortcomings of prior methods based on graph convolutions or their approximations. By stacking layers in which nodes are able to attend over their neighborhoods' features, we enable (implicitly) specifying different weights to different nodes in a neighborhood, without requiring any kind of costly matrix operation (such as inversion) or depending on knowing the graph structure upfront. In this way, we address several key challenges of spectral-based graph neural networks simultaneously, and make our model readily applicable to inductive as well as transductive problems. Our GAT models have achieved or matched state-of-the-art results across four established transductive and inductive graph benchmarks: the Cora, Citeseer and Pubmed citation network datasets, as well as a protein-protein interaction dataset (wherein test graphs remain unseen during training).
